pronunciation.title: Pronunciation dictionary
pronunciation.help: Teach the engine how to say tricky words. Each entry replaces a word with a respelling before synthesis, so it applies on every engine. Scope an entry to one language or leave it Global. Inline, [[word|respelling]] overrides the dictionary once.
pronunciation.empty: No entries yet. Add one above to fix a mispronounced word.
pronunciation.test_placeholder: Type a sentence to preview the substitution…
pronunciation.test_nochange: No entries match — spoken as written.
pronunciation.lang_label: Language code (e.g. en, de) or leave blank for Global

update.install_hint: Download the update and restart into the new version
update.busy: Finish your dub first — then install the update.

updates.backup_line: Your data is backed up before every update.
updates.backup_none: The first backup is created automatically before the next database upgrade.

stories.title: Stories Editor
stories.subtitle: Multi-track audiobook with per-character voices
stories.castTitle: Cast — assign a voice to each character
stories.noProfiles: No voice profiles installed yet — clone or design a voice first.
stories.autocastHint: Detect who's speaking — tagged ([Name]), screenplay (NAME:), or quoted prose — and assign voices automatically
stories.autocastEmpty: Nothing to auto-cast — paste or import a story first.
stories.mp3Fallback: MP3 encode unavailable — downloaded WAV instead.
stories.noProjects: No saved stories yet — Save to keep this one.
stories.splitPlaceholder: Paste a long passage. We'll split it into segments at sentence boundaries.
stories.emptyText: Start your story by adding dialogue and narration lines. Assign each character a voice in the Cast.
stories.linePlaceholder: Enter dialogue or narration… use [pause 0.5s] for a silent break
stories.inlineVoiceHint: Switch voice for the highlighted text (or insert a switch at the cursor)
stories.global_speed_hint: Reading speed for all lines without their own speed override
stories.exportFailed: Export failed — check the backend is running.

audiobook.title: Audiobook
audiobook.subtitle: Turn a chapter-delimited script into a chapterized m4b audiobook.
audiobook.script_placeholder:
# Chapter One
Once upon a time… [pause 500ms] the end.

# Chapter Two
[voice:narrator] And so it continued.
audiobook.failed_note: {{count}} chapter(s) failed — click Create again to retry just those.

launchpad.hero_desc: Clone a voice, design a new one, or dub a video into <1>{{count}} languages</1>. Built for creators who care how it sounds.
launchpad.clone_desc: Drop in a short clip — we'll mirror it. One sample is usually enough.
launchpad.design_desc: Build a new voice from a sentence. Gender, age, accent, mood — your call.
launchpad.dub_desc: Transcribe, translate, re-voice. Keep each speaker, line up the timing, ship it.
launchpad.stories_desc: Multi-voice audiobooks — cast your characters, drop in a script, export by chapter.
launchpad.audiobook_desc: Turn a long script or EPUB into a chaptered audiobook — covers, metadata, one narrator.
launchpad.gallery_desc: Browse ready-made designed voices by accent, age and style — no setup.
launchpad.ab_compare_title: Try two voices side by side
launchpad.empty_hint: Nothing here yet — pick a card above.
launchpad.demo_callout: Try the demo voice — hit Generate to hear it.
launchpad.transcripts_desc: Turn audio or video into editable, searchable text — across 646 languages.

clone.seed_reroll_hint: Roll a new random seed and keep it
clone.drop_audio: Drop audio here — or click. WAV, MP3, M4A…
clone.design_placeholder: Describe the voice, then type what it says…
clone.describe_placeholder: e.g. a warm elderly British storyteller, slightly raspy
clone.describe_hint: Your description sets the controls below — you can still fine-tune them afterwards.
clone.describe_no_match: No voice traits recognized yet — try words like deep, elderly, or British.

settings.title: Settings
settings.autoplay_preview_label: Play the output as soon as a render finishes
settings.autoplay_preview_hint: When off, a finished render no longer starts playing on its own — useful when batch-generating segments.
settings.appearance_help: These controls used to live in the bottom logs bar — moved here so the footer can stay focused on logs. Changes apply instantly and persist across launches.
settings.llmp_desc: Powers Cinematic & Autofit translation — the LLM rewrites each line to fit its segment's time budget so the video timing holds. Keys are stored encrypted; local providers (Ollama/LM Studio) stay fully offline.
settings.llmp_provider_hint: Pick a provider to configure. The active one is used for Cinematic/Autofit translation. Local providers need no key but require their server to be running.
settings.llmp_key_env: set via environment (.env) — overrides this field
settings.llmp_err_config: Missing API key or Base URL — fill them in and save.
settings.llmp_err_auth: Key rejected (401/403) — check your API key.
settings.llmp_err_not_found: Not found (404) — check the model name and Base URL path.
settings.llmp_err_rate_limit: Rate-limited (429) — the key works; try again in a moment.
settings.llmp_err_network: Can't reach the provider — check the Base URL, your network, or that the local server is running.
settings.llmp_env_override: set via an environment variable — overrides this field
settings.llmp_active_env_pin: Active provider is pinned by the LLM_DEFAULT_PROVIDER environment variable.
settings.llmp_models_truncated: first {{count}} shown — type to narrow the model name
settings.proxy_desc: HTTP/SOCKS5 proxy for downloads (yt-dlp, HuggingFace). Supports http://, https://, socks5://. Restart required if changed after backend start.
settings.proxy_saved: Proxy set — yt-dlp will use it for downloads
settings.ffmpeg_desc: Set a custom ffmpeg path if auto-detection fails.
settings.ffmpeg_saved: FFmpeg path set — restart backend to apply.
settings.diagnostics_copied: Diagnostics copied — paste into your issue report.
settings.updater_desktop: Updater only runs in the desktop app.
settings.clear_frontend_confirm: Clear the in-memory frontend log buffer?
settings.clear_tauri_confirm: Truncate the Tauri-side log files? The OS will continue to write new entries.
settings.nothing_to_clear: Nothing to clear — no Tauri log file on disk yet.
settings.clear_backend_confirm: Clear the backend runtime + crash logs? This cannot be undone.
settings.hf_token_desc: Resolved across three sources in priority order — App, Env, HF CLI.
settings.hf_source_app_help: Stored encrypted in OmniVoice's local SQLite store. Set or clear here.
settings.hf_source_env_help: Set via HF_TOKEN in your shell. Read-only from the UI.
settings.hf_source_cli_help: Written by `huggingface-cli login`. Read-only from the UI.
settings.credentials_desc: API keys and tokens, set for this session.
settings.network_desc: Proxy and FFmpeg paths for downloads and media processing.
settings.translation_desc: How dubbing translates dialogue, and which engine does it.
settings.translate_quality_desc: Fast is literal and quick; Cinematic uses the LLM for natural phrasing.
settings.translation_providers_desc: API keys for the DeepL and Microsoft online translators. Saved and restored across restarts.
settings.translation_llm_desc: Cinematic and Autofit translation use a high-quality LLM.
settings.translation_llm_row_desc: Choose, test, and activate a provider (OpenAI, OpenRouter, Groq, a local Ollama, …) in LLM Providers. Keys are stored encrypted; local providers stay fully offline.
settings.review_mode_desc: Pause between pipeline stages so you can review ASR / translation output.
settings.header_live_stats: Show live system metrics in header
settings.header_live_stats_desc: Adds a live RAM / CPU / VRAM monitor to the top bar (off by default).
settings.storage_desc: Where OmniVoice keeps your data and outputs.
settings.factory_reset_desc: Reset all in-app preferences to their defaults. Your files stay untouched.
settings.factory_reset_body: Clears locally-saved settings (theme, language, dub knobs, gallery favorites, and other UI preferences). It does NOT delete your voices, projects, or generated audio on disk.
settings.factory_reset_confirm_body: This clears all saved UI preferences and reloads the app. Your voices, projects, and outputs on disk are not affected. Continue?
settings.storage_usage_desc: What OmniVoice stores on this machine, and how much space is left.
settings.storage_manage_models_hint: Reclaim space by removing models in the Model Store
settings.storage_clear_logs_hint: Truncate the backend runtime and crash logs
settings.storage_warn_critical: Critically low disk space: {{free}} GB free on {{path}} — OmniVoice needs at least {{min}} GB. Generation and model downloads may fail.
settings.storage_warn_low: Low disk space: {{free}} GB free on {{path}} — getting close to the {{min}} GB minimum.
settings.storage_warn_unreadable: Could not fully scan {{path}} — the size shown may be incomplete.

about.channel_preview_hint: Preview tracks the latest main build — newer features, less tested. Falls back to stable if a stable release is ahead.
about.self_check_healthy: All checks passed — this install looks healthy.
about.self_check_attention: {{count}} check(s) failed — see the hints above.
about.bundle_failed: Could not build the diagnostic bundle: {{message}}

privacy.desc: Everything runs on <1>this machine</1>. Your audio, video, and transcripts never leave your computer unless you explicitly use an online translator (Google, DeepL, etc.) or push to HuggingFace.

credentials.title: Credentials
credentials.desc: API keys and tokens are set <1>for this session only</1>. For persistence across restarts, set them as environment variables in your shell profile.
credentials.hf_help: Required for speaker diarization and faster model downloads. Get yours at huggingface.co/settings/tokens.
credentials.translate_help: Optional — for DeepL, OpenAI, or paid translation providers. Not needed for Google Translate (free tier).
credentials.deepl_help: For DeepL translation engine. Get your key at deepl.com/pro-api.
credentials.deepl_base_url_help: Custom DeepL API endpoint (default: https://api.deepl.com/v2). Change for proxy/self-hosted.
credentials.microsoft_help: For Microsoft Translator engine. Get your key from Azure Portal.
credentials.llm_base_url_help: For Cinematic quality or LLM engine. Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (OpenAI, Ollama, LM Studio, etc.).
credentials.llm_model_help: Model name to use with the LLM engine (e.g. gpt-4o, deepseek-chat, qwen2.5).

capture.desc: Global hotkeys only work in the desktop app. The web UI uses an in-page <1>Ctrl+Shift+Space</1> shortcut while the window has focus.
capture.desc_detail: The hotkey works system-wide while OmniVoice is running — it focuses the window and starts dictation. Avoid combos already claimed by the OS (on macOS, <1>⌘+Space</1> is Spotlight and <2>⌘+⇧+Space</2> cycles input sources). If registration fails, pick a different combo.
capture.a11y_setup: Allow Accessibility so dictation can type for you
capture.paste_error: Couldn't paste into the active app
capture.mic_hint_mac: macOS: open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Microphone and enable OmniVoice.
capture.mic_hint_windows: Windows: open Settings → Privacy & security → Microphone, turn on 'Microphone access' and 'Let desktop apps access your microphone', then allow OmniVoice.
capture.mic_hint_linux: Linux: check that your user is in the audio group and the WebView has mic access.
capture.mic_not_found: No microphone found. Connect or enable a microphone and try again.
capture.mic_in_use: The microphone is busy or unavailable. Close other apps that may be using it and try again.

voicePanel.title: Voice
voicePanel.subtitle: Local speech-to-text dictation with on-device models.
voicePanel.enable_sub: Press {{shortcut}} to dictate text into any focused pane.
voicePanel.mode_sub: Toggle: press once to start, again to stop. Hold: dictate while the key is held.
voicePanel.model_sub: Pick the on-device model used for dictation.
voicePanel.delete_confirm: Delete the "{{label}}" speech model? You can re-download it later.
voicePanel.engine_unavailable: The live-dictation engine isn't available on this install. Dictation falls back to the standard transcription path.

model_desc.sherpa-parakeet-tdt-v3: Recommended. Fast, accurate dictation across 25 European languages.
model_desc.sherpa-zipformer-bilingual-zh-en: Streaming Chinese + English with live partials.
model_desc.sherpa-zipformer-en-20m: Tiny streaming English model — lowest latency.
model_desc.sherpa-zipformer-zh-14m: Tiny streaming Chinese model — lowest latency.
model_desc.sherpa-whisper-tiny: Multilingual (90+ languages) with auto language detection.

profiles.need_name_audio: Need a name and reference audio
profiles.locked: Voice locked! Identity is now consistent across all generations.
profiles.unlocked: Voice unlocked. Generations will vary again.

voice_profile.persona_export_no_audio: This voice has no readable audio to build a preview from.
voice_profile.persona_include_ref_hint: Include the raw reference clip. Off = share only a watermarked preview (recommended).
voice_profile.consent_explain: Record yourself reading the statement below to verify this is your own voice. Verification will be required for agentic features and community sharing — never for local synthesis.
voice_profile.consent_statement: I confirm that this voice profile is my own voice, and I consent to OmniVoice Studio cloning it on my behalf.
voice_profile.consent_verified_explain: You recorded a consent statement on {{date}}. Revoking removes the recording and the verified status.
voice_profile.consent_revoke_confirm: Revoke voice-ownership verification? The consent recording will be deleted and agentic features will no longer be able to use this voice.
voice_profile.test_text: Hello — this is a test of this voice.
voice_profile.delete_confirm: Delete "{{name}}" permanently? This also removes the reference audio on disk.
voice_profile.unlock_confirm: Unlock this voice? Future generations will no longer be bit-reproducible.
voice_profile.ref_help: What the reference audio says. Used to improve clone accuracy.
voice_profile.locked_explain: This voice is bit-reproducible. Every generation uses the same reference + seed.
voice_profile.test_help: Type anything — we'll generate it with this voice's current settings.
voice_profile.test_placeholder: Type something to hear this voice say it…
voice_profile.used_title: Where this voice has been used
voice_profile.used_empty: This voice hasn't been used yet. Generate a preview above, or pick it in a dub.

dub.install_popover_frozen_body: This packaged build can't install engines — its Python environment is read-only and signed. Run the command below in a from-source install, or switch to the bundled Argos engine.
dub.speaker: Speaker {{n}}:
dub.dialect_title: Regional dialect and vocabulary for the translation (e.g. Argentina → "vos sos" instead of "tú eres"). Applied by LLM engines (OpenAI/Ollama) and Cinematic quality.
dub.num_speakers_help: How many speakers are in this video? Leave blank to auto-detect. Set a number if auto-detect merges multiple speakers into one.
dub.prep_demucs_note: Demucs can take several minutes on long videos. Long audio = longer wait.
dub.supported_formats: MP4 · MOV · MKV · WEBM · MP3 · WAV · FLAC · M4A
dub.pull_captions_title: When the URL is a caption-bearing host (YouTube, Vimeo, TED…), also pull the original captions and any YouTube auto-translations. Seeds the editor without running Whisper; skip Translate All for languages YouTube already covers.
dub.first_play_hint: first play may take a moment to mux
dub.cast_title: Assign a voice to each detected speaker. Cross-lingual clones keep the same speaker identity in a new language.
dub.quality_title: Cinematic = 3-step LLM refinement (translate → reflect → adapt). Needs an LLM configured.
dub.restore_title: Restore all segments to the original transcribed text
dub.clean_up_title: Merge tiny fragments and adjacent short segments
dub.install_disabled_title: Installs are disabled in packaged builds
dub.glossary_title: Pin translations for recurring terms (names, brand words, jargon)
dub.dual_subs_title: Export subtitles with translated text on top and original italicised underneath.
dub.burn_subs_title: Render subtitles directly into the MP4 video stream (hardsubs). Uses the dual-subtitle format when Dual subtitles is on.
dub.timing_smart_fit_title: Splits the difference: slightly speeds up the audio (pitch preserved, up to 1.5×) and slightly slows down that segment of the video (up to 2×) so natural-rate speech fits. Anything beyond the caps is trimmed and flagged. Export re-encodes the video.
dub.qc_result: {{flagged}} of {{total}} lines may need a re-listen
dub.install_restart: {{engine}} installed. Restart the backend to load it.
dub.cinematic_needs_llm_hint: Cinematic needs an LLM. Configure one in Settings → Credentials → LLM endpoint (Ollama runs locally, no key needed).
dub.hq_needs_llm_hint: High-quality translation fits each line to its segment time using a local or cloud LLM. Set one up to enable it.

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.clear_auto_confirm: Remove all auto-extracted terms? Manual entries are kept.
glossary.auto_added_one: Added {{count}} auto term. Review and edit before translating.
glossary.auto_added_other: Added {{count}} auto terms. Review and edit before translating.
glossary.auto_title: Ask the LLM to scan segments for proper nouns + recurring terms
glossary.clear_auto_title: Remove all auto-extracted terms, keep manual ones
glossary.empty_save: Save the dub as a project to start a glossary.
glossary.no_terms: No terms yet. Add one below or click Auto.
glossary.hint: Glossary is injected into every Cinematic translate prompt. Manual entries take precedence.

segment.sync_title: Generated audio is {{pct}}% the duration of original
segment.budget_title: Text is {{pct}}% of original — consider higher speed or shorter phrasing
segment.text_title: Ctrl+D to split at cursor · Ctrl+M to merge with next
segment.speaker_title_detected: Speaker — pick from detected, or type a custom name
segment.speaker_title_custom: Speaker — type a name (no diarization clones detected)
segment.time_edit_title: Click to edit start time (m:ss.s). Enter to commit, Esc to cancel.
segment.qc_verify_title: Second-pass ASR heard: "{{heard}}" — re-listen or re-dub this line.
segment.fit_fits_title: Natural-rate audio fit inside the slot.
segment.fit_overflows_title: Translated text was longer than the original slot by {{seconds}}s. The audio was hard-trimmed; shorten the text or switch Timing to "Stretch Video".
segment.fit_stretched_title: Stretch Video mode: this segment's video was slowed to {{ratio}}× to fit the natural dub audio.
segment.fit_compressed_title: TTS audio is {{pct}}% of the slot — heavily compressed.
segment.fit_ratio_title: TTS audio is {{pct}}% of the slot.

timeline.keyboard_hint: Arrow keys select a segment · Enter toggles edit mode · Delete removes · S snaps to the nearest speech onset
timeline.overlap_warning: Overlaps an adjacent segment — both lines will play together
timeline.snapped_announce: Edge snapped to speech onset at {{time}}
timeline.edit_mode_on: Edit mode: arrows nudge the start, Shift adjusts the end, Alt moves the segment, Esc finishes

tools.title: Tools
tools.desc: Audio processing, format conversion, and utilities
tools.directorial_desc: Type a natural-language direction — see the taxonomy tokens it maps to + the TTS instruct + translate hint + speech-rate bias.
tools.speech_rate_desc: Check how a translated line compares to its time slot.
tools.probe_desc: ffprobe wrapper — inspect codec, streams, bit depth, duration.

projects.title: Projects
projects.empty_hint: Nothing here yet. Start a dub, design a voice, or generate audio to see it appear.

batch.title: Batch dubbing
batch.no_failed: No failed jobs — enjoy the silence.
batch.drop_hint: Drop videos above to start batch dubbing.
batch.drop_hint_text: Drop video files here or click to browse

gallery.title: OmniVoice Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Hundreds of ready-made designed voices — pick one and go.
gallery.persona_too_large: That bundle is too large (max 100 MB).
gallery.persona_import_failed: Could not import that persona bundle.
gallery.use_failed: Could not create that voice — the engine may be loading.
gallery.preview_failed: Preview unavailable — the voice engine may still be loading.
gallery.import_explainer: Paste a URL you have the rights to (or upload a file), trim the part you need, and save it as a voice. You are responsible for the licensing of anything you import.
gallery.import_placeholder: Paste a video/audio URL, or type to search…
gallery.no_imports: Nothing imported yet. Paste a URL above to get started.
gallery.trim_load_failed: Could not load audio for trimming.
gallery.community_empty: No community voices loaded yet — connect to the internet and reopen, or be the first to submit one.
gallery.community_explainer: Designed presets and recorded voices shared by the community, loaded from the omnivoice-gallery.
gallery.no_preview: No preview — add it with "Use voice" to hear it.
gallery.submit_failed: Could not open the submission form.

transcriptions.title: Transcriptions
transcriptions.empty_desc: Use the capture button (⌘+⇧+Space) to record and transcribe audio.

transcriptionPicker.title: Import from a transcription
transcriptionPicker.empty: No transcriptions yet. Record one in the Transcriptions tab.

setup.system_check_desc: Probe RAM, disk, GPU, ffmpeg, and network. Blockers are flagged upfront so you know before downloading.
setup.install_models_desc: Download ~5 GB of weights — TTS + Whisper. Required models first, optional ones later.
setup.pick_engines_desc: Choose TTS / ASR / LLM backends. Defaults work out of the box — customize anytime in Settings.
setup.hero_desc: Dubbing, voice cloning, and voice design — all running locally on your machine.
setup.first_run: First run takes 5–10 minutes to download. After that, every launch is instant and fully offline.
setup.resolve_blockers: Resolve the failing checks above to continue.
setup.install_required_models: Install the required models above to continue.

donate.title: Support
donate.desc: If you enjoy this project, consider donating to support our development.
donate.hero_desc: OmniVoice is free, open-source, and runs entirely on your hardware. If it brings value to your workflow, consider supporting the core team.
donate.github_desc: Recurring or one-time — directly through GitHub.
donate.coffee_desc: Buy the team a coffee. No account needed.
donate.paypal_desc: Quick one-time or recurring via PayPal.
donate.footer: Every contribution helps push the boundaries of local AI.

postcard.title: Fund Claude Max
postcard.lead_default: Glad that worked! OmniVoice is free and fully local. If it saves you time, a small monthly chip-in funds the Claude Max behind it.
postcard.lead_first_clone: Your first voice clone is done — nice! OmniVoice runs entirely on your machine, and your support keeps it that way.
postcard.lead_tenth_dub: Ten dubs in — you're clearly putting it to work. A small monthly chip-in funds the Claude Max that ships these features.
postcard.lead_sustained: You've been with OmniVoice for a month. If it's earned a spot in your workflow, consider helping fund what's next.

enterprise.title: Commercial License
enterprise.desc: Professional licensing and technical support for commercial users.
enterprise.hero_simple: OmniVoice Studio is free and open-source under the AGPL-3.0 — including for commercial and internal business use. You only need a commercial license to embed it in a closed-source product without AGPL’s copyleft obligations.
enterprise.contact_lead: Pricing is quoted per deployment so it fits your team and workload. Tell me your use case and I’ll get you a quote.
enterprise.hero_desc: OmniVoice Studio is free and open-source software under the GNU Affero General Public License v3 (AGPL-3.0) — free to use, including for commercial and internal business use. A commercial license is needed only if you want to embed OmniVoice Studio in a closed-source or proprietary product or service without AGPL-3.0's copyleft obligations.
enterprise.hero_note: Use, self-hosting, and commercial use are all free under the AGPL-3.0 — including at scale. AGPL is a network-copyleft license: if you modify OmniVoice and offer that modified version to others over a network, you must share your modified source under the same terms. A commercial license lifts those copyleft obligations for proprietary, closed-source deployments. Pricing tiers are coming soon — get in touch in the meantime.
enterprise.pricing_desc: Tiers and pricing are still being finalized.
enterprise.pricing_detail: Until they’re public, every commercial deployment is being quoted individually so we can right-size for your team and workload.
enterprise.footer_discord: Join our Discord for community support.
enterprise.benefit_ip_desc: You own every output, every model, every derivative. No shared tenancy, no data leaks.
enterprise.benefit_cost_desc: No API bills, no surprise overages. Runs on your own hardware from day one.
enterprise.benefit_team_desc: One license covers unlimited users within your organization.
enterprise.benefit_support_desc: Priority email and video-call support from the core engineering team.
enterprise.benefit_source_desc: Full visibility into the stack. Audit, fork, and adapt within the license terms.
enterprise.benefit_lang_desc: Transcribe, translate, and dub across 646 languages with human-level quality.

exportModal.default_audio_hint: Which audio stream plays by default when the viewer opens the file
exportModal.mix_bg_video: Mix music/FX under every dubbed track
exportModal.dual_subs_video: Dual (translated on top of italicised original)
exportModal.mix_bg_audio: Mix music/FX under the dubbed voice
exportModal.subs_note: Subtitles are generated from segment text as-is — edit the segment table before exporting if you spotted typos.
exportModal.retime_note: Per-segment video re-timing re-encodes the entire video on export — expect it to take roughly 0.5–2× the video's length on CPU.
exportModal.pkg_clips_body: Every generated segment as a numbered WAV inside a zip — good for review, voice-over post, or dataset building.
exportModal.pkg_stems_body: Isolated vocal track + background (music/FX) as separate WAVs. Useful for downstream audio editing.
exportModal.pkg_audio_body_one: Jump to the Audio tab to export per-language dubs in WAV or MP3 ({{count}} dub available).
exportModal.pkg_audio_body_other: Jump to the Audio tab to export per-language dubs in WAV or MP3 ({{count}} dubs available).

demo.clone_coachmark: This is a guided demo — try the suggested prompt or write your own.
demo.prerendered_chip: Pre-rendered sample — install a TTS engine to synthesize new text.
demo.dictation_lede: Read one of these aloud after pressing your hotkey, or hit Replay to send the bundled sample through the transcriber so you can see dictation work end-to-end without speaking.
demo.dictation_status_ok: Verified — hotkey works on this machine
demo.dictation_status_pending: Press your shortcut anywhere to test
demo.dictation_status_warn: No hotkey registered — set one below
demo.dubbing_cta: Run this on your own video →
demo.dictation_lede_hotkey_only: Hold the shortcut above anywhere on your desktop, speak, release — the text lands in whatever app has focus. Press it now to verify it works.

bootstrap.title: OmniVoice Studio
bootstrap.clean_retry_confirm: This will delete the cached Python environment and re-download all dependencies (~5-10 min). Continue?
bootstrap.hint_readme: README.md was missing from the bundle. This is now auto-fixed — retry should work.
bootstrap.hint_python_mirror: Network couldn't reach the Python download (github.com release). Switch your region in Settings → Network (China / Russia / Restricted route through a mirror), or install Python 3.11+ system-wide so the app uses that instead.
bootstrap.hint_uv_timeout: Network timeout downloading uv. Check your internet connection or try a different region in Settings → Network.
bootstrap.hint_uv_sync: Dependency install failed. "Clean & Retry" will delete the cached venv and start fresh.
bootstrap.hint_build_backend: Python build backend error. "Clean & Retry" removes the broken venv so it rebuilds from scratch.
bootstrap.hint_ffmpeg: ffmpeg download failed. This is non-fatal — retry or install ffmpeg manually.
bootstrap.hint_port: Port 3900 is already in use. Close other instances of OmniVoice or apps using that port.
bootstrap.hint_silent_crash: Backend crashed silently. "Clean & Retry" often fixes corrupt venv issues.
bootstrap.hint_github_blocked: Your network may block GitHub. Install Python 3.11+ from python.org (Add to PATH) and relaunch, or set UV_PYTHON_INSTALL_MIRROR — see docs/install/troubleshooting.md.
bootstrap.hint_intel_mac: This Mac has an Intel processor, and PyTorch no longer ships Intel-Mac builds — the local AI backend can't be installed, and retrying won't change that. You can still point the app at a remote backend running on another machine (Settings → Sharing → Remote backend), or run OmniVoice on an Apple Silicon Mac, Windows, or Linux. Details: docs/install/macos.md.
bootstrap.hint_stuck: The backend didn't finish starting. From source, run `uv sync` first and make sure `uv` and Python are on your PATH, then Retry. Check the log below (or Settings → Logs → Backend) for the exact stall point.
bootstrap.hint_default: Try "Retry" first. If it fails again, "Clean & Retry" will rebuild the environment from scratch.
bootstrap.ipc_lost_body: The window lost its connection to the app's core (Tauri IPC isn't responding) and the local backend hasn't answered yet. This usually happens when the WebView cache was corrupted by a crash or forced shutdown. The app keeps checking in the background and will continue automatically if the backend comes up.
bootstrap.ipc_lost_log_hint: Couldn't open the folder automatically. Logs are at: {{path}}
bootstrap.ipc_lost_repair_confirm: This clears the app's WebView cache (display data only — your voices, projects, and settings are untouched) and restarts OmniVoice Studio. Continue?
bootstrap.ipc_lost_repair_failed: Automatic repair didn't work. Quit OmniVoice Studio, delete this folder, then start the app again: {{path}}

direction.title: Direction for segment #{{id}}
direction.desc: Tell the pipeline how this line should feel. Plain English works — the system maps your words onto a stable taxonomy (energy / emotion / pace / intimacy / formality), then threads the taxonomy through Cinematic translation, TTS, and slot-fit.
direction.label: Direction
direction.placeholder: e.g. urgent and surprised  /  warm, hopeful  /  whispered, intimate

engines.subprocessTitle: Runs in its own subprocess + venv
engines.inProcessTitle: Runs in the OmniVoice Python process

errors.title: This tab hit a snag.
errors.desc: Don't worry — the rest of the app still works. You can switch tabs, or try again below.

keyboard.title: Keyboard shortcuts
keyboard.footer: Press <1>?</1> any time to open this.

network.share_confirm_hint: Other devices on your Wi-Fi/Ethernet will be able to reach OmniVoice using the access PIN shown once it's on.
network.no_interface: No reachable network interface — connect to Wi-Fi/Ethernet.

readiness.loading_first_run: Loading… (this may take 1-2 minutes on first run)
readiness.not_loaded_yet: Not loaded yet — will load on first transcription
readiness.error_check_logs: Error: {{error}}. Check logs and try restarting.
readiness.check_logs_restart: Check logs for model loading errors. Try restarting.
readiness.llm_configure: Configure TRANSLATE_BASE_URL for Cinematic translation quality
readiness.llm_set_env: Set TRANSLATE_BASE_URL and TRANSLATE_API_KEY environment variables. Works with Ollama, OpenAI, LM Studio, etc.
readiness.llm_optional: Optional — set TRANSLATE_BASE_URL for Cinematic quality

license.title: Supertonic-3 — License Acceptance
license.intro: Supertonic-3 ships under two distinct licenses. Please review both before enabling the engine.
license.sdk_desc: The Python inference SDK (supertonic) is MIT-licensed. Permissive use, including commercial.
license.model_desc: The Supertonic-3 model weights are released under the OpenRAIL-M license. This license restricts use to non-malicious purposes — see the linked license for the full set of use-based restrictions.
license.footer: Clicking Accept records your acceptance in OmniVoice's local settings and enables the engine. Your acceptance is stored on this machine only — nothing is reported to Supertone Inc. or any third party.

voicePreview.title: Voice Preview
voicePreview.default_text: Hello! This is a preview of how I sound in this voice.

sidebar.no_dub_hint: Upload a video and click Save to keep your work.
sidebar.no_clones_hint: Record or upload audio, then click Save as Voice Profile.
sidebar.no_designs_hint: Generate a voice and save it from History.
sidebar.no_history_hint: Synthesize audio or dub a video — results will appear here.
sidebar.clear_confirm: Clear all {{count}} history items? This cannot be undone.
sidebar.no_exports_hint: Export a file via Tauri to see it tracked here.

logs.no_tauri_log: No Tauri log on disk yet — launch via the desktop build to produce one
logs.empty_frontend: No frontend console entries captured yet. Interact with the app — every console.* will appear here.
logs.empty_tauri: No Tauri log available. Runs in the desktop shell only.
logs.empty_backend: Runtime log is empty. Activity will appear here as the backend logs it.
logs.title: Logs
logs.report_copied: Diagnostic report copied — paste it into a GitHub issue.

trimmer.title: Trim reference audio
trimmer.keyboard_hint: scroll = zoom · shift+scroll = pan · alt+drag = pan · ⏐ ⟵ ⟶ ⏐ keys adjust handles
trimmer.play_hint: Space to play · Enter to confirm · Esc to cancel

waveform.source_missing: Source media missing — this file may have moved or been deleted. Re-upload the video to continue.
waveform.load_failed: Could not load audio from this file

casting.title: Speaker Casting
casting.auto_assign_title: Auto-assign voices from extracted speaker clones

checkpoint.asr_hint: Fix any ASR errors now — tight diction saves TTS attempts later.
checkpoint.translate_hint: Skim the target text. Over-length lines get speed-boosted; you can also edit directly.
checkpoint.done_hint: Review timing and sync ratios. Tweak any line and hit "Regen changed" for a fast partial redo.
checkpoint.dismiss_title: Dismiss — won't reappear for this stage until reload

compare.title: A/B Voice Comparison
compare.desc: Compare two voices side by side to make casting decisions. App stays interactive behind.

models.hf_token_set_toast: HuggingFace token set — faster downloads enabled
models.install_started: Install started — progress in the row
models.delete_confirm: Delete {{repoId}}? You can reinstall it later.
models.reinstall_confirm: Reinstall {{repoId}}? This will delete the current copy and download again.
models.fast_download_title: Fast downloads via Xet {{version}} — parallel chunked transfer
models.disk_free_title: Free space on the model-cache volume
models.hf_set_title: Set HuggingFace token for faster downloads
models.mirror_description: Route model downloads through a mirror on a restricted network.
models.mirror_preset_hint: On a restricted network, route model downloads through a mirror. Leave empty for the official endpoint.
models.mirror_restart_note: Model Store downloads use the new mirror immediately. Only model loads (transformers) pick it up after a restart.

enterprise_faq.q_internal_tools: Do I need a license for internal tools?
enterprise_faq.a_internal_tools: No. Use by your employees and contractors — including modifying and self-hosting internally — is free under the AGPL-3.0. A commercial license is only needed if you embed OmniVoice in a closed-source or proprietary product or service and don't want to comply with AGPL's source-sharing obligations.
enterprise_faq.a_try_before: Yes. The full app is free to download, run, and self-host under the AGPL-3.0 — no agreement required. When you're ready to discuss a commercial (proprietary-use) license, email us and we'll work through the details together.
enterprise_faq.a_watermark: The invisible AudioSeal watermark is embedded by default for everyone. Commercial licensees can disable it in Settings → Privacy.

gallery_extra.save_prompt: Enter a name for this voice profile:
gallery_extra.trim_load_failed: Failed to load audio for trimming: {{message}}

dub_workflow.session_expired: This dub session expired or was cleaned up — re-upload your video to start a new one.
dub_workflow.import_srt_no_job: Upload or ingest a video first — there is no job to attach subtitles to.
dub_workflow.cinematic_no_llm: Cinematic quality needs an LLM. Configure one in Settings → Credentials → LLM endpoint (Ollama runs locally, no key needed). Using Fast quality for now.
dub_workflow.dialect_not_applied: The selected dialect needs an LLM to apply. Switch the translation engine to OpenAI/Ollama, or configure an LLM in Settings → Credentials → LLM endpoint.
dub_workflow.save_first: Please click 'Upload & Transcribe' first so the video is processed on the server before saving.
dub_workflow.delete_project_confirm: Delete this project? This cannot be undone.
dub_workflow.upgrading_preview: Upgrading {{count}} preview-quality segment(s) to full quality…

tts_errors.upload_or_select: Upload an audio or select a voice profile
tts_errors.trim_hint: Audio is {{duration}}s — trim to ≤{{max}}s for best cloning
tts_errors.timeout: Generation timed out — the model may still be downloading. Check Settings → Logs, then try again.

tts.routingFallback: Running on CPU — this engine has no GPU path on your machine, so generation will be slower. {{reason}}

sharing.title: Sharing & Remote Access
sharing.help: Expose this running OmniVoice instance to your other machines without restarting it. Loopback-only is the default — nothing is shared until you turn it on here.
sharing.local_help: Share on your Wi-Fi / Ethernet with a one-time access PIN. Other devices scan the QR code or open the link.
sharing.ports_help: These are set via environment variables read at startup. Change the backend or UI port by setting the variable and restarting OmniVoice.
sharing.port_saved: LAN-share port saved — applies next time you enable sharing
sharing.ports_note: Backend and UI ports apply on restart. The LAN-share port applies next time you enable sharing.
sharing.tailscale_absent: Tailscale not detected. Install it to reach OmniVoice securely from anywhere on your private tailnet.
sharing.tailscale_running: Tailscale is running. Serve OmniVoice over your private tailnet.
sharing.tailscale_not_logged_in: Tailscale is installed but not logged in. Start and sign in to Tailscale first.
sharing.tailscale_qr_alt: QR code for the Tailscale URL

remote_gate.title: Enter access PIN
remote_gate.body: This OmniVoice instance is shared on the network. Enter the PIN shown on the host.
remote_gate.label: Access PIN

reportBug.label: Report a bug
reportBug.title: Opens a prefilled GitHub Issues page in your browser. Nothing is sent until you click Submit.

app.toast_upload_first: Please click 'Upload & Transcribe' first so the video is processed on the server before saving.

footer_donate.line_1: That export was 100% local — no cloud, no fees. If OmniVoice saves you time, a coffee keeps it alive.
footer_donate.line_2: Another one finished, entirely on your machine. OmniVoice is one developer plus supporters like you — a small tip goes a long way.
footer_donate.line_3: You just made that with zero API keys and zero subscriptions. If it was worth a coffee, that keeps the updates coming.
footer_donate.line_4: Glad that worked! OmniVoice stays free and open source — and supporters are what keep it that way.

contact.hero_desc: Questions, bugs, licensing, or just to say hi — here’s how to reach me.
contact.discord_desc: Chat with the community and get help fast.
contact.issues_desc: Report a bug or request a feature.
contact.website_desc: More about the project and the maker.

firstrun.title: Set up OmniVoice Studio
firstrun.subtitle: Nothing's installed yet — review where everything goes, then start. Change it later in Settings.
firstrun.mode_portable_desc: Everything lives in one folder next to the app — move it to another disk or machine as a unit.
firstrun.mode_portable_unavailable: Unavailable: the folder next to the app is not writable.
firstrun.portable_folder_desc: App environment, models, and your voice data — one folder, fully movable.
firstrun.data_dir_desc: Your voices, dubs, outputs and project database.
firstrun.models_dir_desc: Downloaded AI models — the largest and most relocatable part.
firstrun.insufficient_space: Not enough free space: this layout needs ~{{need}} on one disk, only {{free}} available. Pick a different location.
firstrun.blocked_not_writable: A chosen folder is not writable — pick a different location.
firstrun.total_required: Total disk needed: ~{{size}} (one-time download on first use)
firstrun.compute_auto_desc: Best backend picked at runtime — CUDA on NVIDIA, MPS on Apple Silicon, else CPU.
firstrun.compute_rocm_desc: Installs PyTorch ROCm wheels for AMD graphics cards on Linux. Leave on Auto if unsure.
firstrun.channel_stable_desc: Tested releases only, after community validation.
firstrun.channel_preview_desc: Latest main — new engines and fixes first, occasional rough edges.
firstrun.trust_line: Everything runs and stays on this machine — no account, no cloud, no telemetry.
firstrun.resume_note: Interrupted downloads resume automatically — closing the app is safe.
firstrun.first_sound_text: Welcome to your studio. Every word you hear was generated on this machine, just now.
firstrun.first_sound_done: That voice? Generated seconds ago, locally. Welcome in.
firstrun.hf_token_title: Hugging Face token — faster, more reliable downloads (optional)
firstrun.hf_token_hint: A free account token gives authenticated downloads (faster, higher rate limits, fewer stalls) and unlocks gated models like speaker diarization for multi-speaker dubbing (pyannote). Stays on this machine.
firstrun.hf_token_card_title: Add a free Hugging Face token for faster downloads
firstrun.hf_token_get: Don't have a token? Get one free →
firstrun.hf_token_saved_fast: Hugging Face token saved — downloads are now faster
firstrun.hf_token_error: Could not save the token — try again or set it later in Settings → Credentials.
firstrun.hf_token_inline_prompt: Speed up downloads with a free Hugging Face token

history.empty: Nothing here yet — your generations will appear on the right.
history.title: History
